description: Base case uses <= 0 instead of <= 1, breaks for n=0 vs n=1
Loop iterates one past the end of the list
correct_code:
for i in range(len(items)):
    process(items[i])

description: Slice excludes the last intended element
correct_code: result = items[:5]

description: Range excludes n when it should include it
correct_code:
for i in range(1, n + 1):
    total += i
buggy_code:
for i in range(1, n):
    total += i

description: Boundary check uses > instead of >=, missing index 0
correct_code: if idx >= 0 and idx < len(arr):

description: Assignment = instead of comparison ==
correct_code: if x == y:

description: Equality check where inequality is needed
correct_code: if a != b:

description: <= causes index out of bounds
correct_code: while i < len(data):

description: Strict > excludes exactly 18
correct_code: if age >= 18:

description: Logical OR instead of AND
correct_code: result = a and b

description: No None check before attribute access
No empty list check before indexing
No type check before numeric operation
correct_code:
if isinstance(value, int):
    total += value

description: No key existence check before dict access
No HTTP status check before parsing
Uses singular variable instead of plural collection
correct_code: total = sum(values)

description: Processes entire collection instead of each item
correct_code:
for item in items:
    process(item)

description: Public attribute instead of private backing field
correct_code: return self._value

description: Duplicate variable instead of different operand
correct_code: result = first + second

description: Typo in method name: tranform vs transform
correct_code: output = input_data.transform()

description: Else clause logic always executes due to indentation
Loop body not indented, only last item processed
elif replaced with if; zero case reached when x > 0 is False
Infinite loop: loop variable never updated
No try/except around potentially failing operation
Modifies original list instead of a copy
correct_code:
new_list = old_list.copy()
new_list.append(item)

description: Mutates input argument instead of returning new list
correct_code:
def add_item(items, new):
    return items + [new]

description: Shallow reference instead of copy
correct_code:
defaults = {'a': 1, 'b': 2}
copy = defaults.copy()

description: In-place sort instead of sorted(), mutates original
correct_code: result = sorted(items, key=lambda x: x.age)

description: Modifies original config dict
correct_code:
config = original.copy()
config['key'] = 'new_value'

description: Coroutine not awaited, result is coroutine object
correct_code: result = await fetch_data()

description: Sequential awaits instead of concurrent gather
No lock around shared mutable state
correct_code:
async with lock:
    shared_data = compute()

description: Two missing awaits on async operations
correct_code:
response = await client.get(url)
data = await response.json()

description: No exception handling in async function
buggy_code:
async def safe_read():
    return await read_file()

description: File not closed, resource leak
correct_code:
with open(path) as f:
    data = f.read()

description: Database cursor not properly closed
correct_code:
with db.cursor() as cur:
    cur.execute(query)

description: Connection not closed in finally block
Executor not shut down
correct_code:
with ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
    results = list(executor.map(fn, data))

description: Temporary file not properly managed
correct_code:
import tempfile
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=True) as tmp:
    tmp.write(data)

description: Binary search: wrong bounds and no increment causes infinite loop
Mutates dict1 instead of creating new merged dict
correct_code:
def merge(dict1, dict2):
    result = dict1.copy()
    result.update(dict2)
    return result

description: Clamp arguments swapped — always returns lo
correct_code:
def clamp(value, lo, hi):
    return max(lo, min(value, hi))
buggy_code:
def clamp(value, lo, hi):
    return min(lo, max(value, hi))

description: No normalization: prevents case/space matching
set() removes duplicates but loses order guarantee
Mutable default argument shared across calls
Mutable dict default shared between calls
Class-level mutable dict shared across instances
[[0]*cols]*rows creates shared inner lists
correct_code:
def build_matrix(rows, cols, fill=0):
    return [[fill for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
buggy_code:
def build_matrix(rows, cols, fill=0):
    return [[fill] * cols] * rows

description: List multiplication creates shared references, modifying one modifies all
correct_code:
results = [[0]*3 for _ in range(3)]
results[0][0] = 1
